metadata.title: GCS A100 Ordered Fallback for L4 Capacity Exhaustion
metadata.priority: P0 — 3 GPU jobs stuck PENDING for 11+ hours, controller burning $3.22/day
metadata.estimated-cost: Implementation: 0 (config changes). Per-pass when A100 used: ~$18.70 vs $6.85 for L4
metadata.user-authorization: Explicit — user requested after reviewing gpu-instances-finops-report.md

expert.focus: SkyPilot ordered: block syntax and failover behavior
expert.assessment:
SkyPilot's `ordered:` block in resources specifies a priority-ordered list of resource configurations. SkyPilot tries each entry in order until one can be provisioned. This is the CORRECT mechanism for L4-to-A100 fallback — it is a 2-line YAML change in the region config, not an architecture change.
Key technical detail: the `ordered:` block is ALREADY used by this project for multi-region failover (see `src/minivess/cloud/region_injection.py`). The region_injection module reads `configs/cloud/regions/*.yaml` and generates `ordered:` blocks with per-region entries. Adding A100 as a GPU fallback means extending the region config format to support MULTIPLE GPU types per region, creating a (GPU, region) matrix in the ordered list.
Current region config structure: ```yaml regions: gcp: L4: - region: europe-west4 zones: 3 ```
Proposed extension: ```yaml regions: gcp: L4: - region: europe-west4 zones: 3 A100: - region: europe-west4 zones: 3 ```
The region_injection module already iterates `for gpu_type, entries in provider_regions.items()` — adding A100 entries requires ZERO code changes to region_injection.py. The ordered list will naturally contain L4 entries first (from config order), then A100 entries.
IMPORTANT: The base `train_factorial.yaml` keeps `accelerators: L4:1` as the static default. The `ordered:` block is INJECTED at launch time by `run_factorial.sh` via the region_injection module. This means the static YAML file itself does NOT change — only the region config and the contract change.
Spot override for SAM3: The `run_factorial.sh` already passes `--no-use-spot` for SAM3 models (via MODEL_USE_SPOT from model_overrides). This flag overrides the `use_spot: true` in the YAML. This mechanism works with both L4 and A100 because it is applied at the `sky jobs launch` command level, not in the YAML.
expert.recommendation: 1. Update region configs to include A100 entries AFTER all L4 entries 2. Update the YAML contract to allow A100 for GCP 3. Do NOT modify train_factorial.yaml itself — the ordered: injection handles it 4. The region_injection.py code needs NO changes (already handles multi-GPU)

expert.focus: Price comparison and total cost per pass
expert.assessment:
Cost analysis for a 34-job debug factorial pass, using data from the FinOps report and verified GCP europe-west4 pricing (March 2026):
ASSUMPTIONS: - Setup time per job: 10 min (Docker pull + DVC data + weight loading) — same region - L4 training time per job: 45 min (observed from prior passes) - A100 speedup factor: 2.2x (conservative for memory-bandwidth-bound 3D segmentation) - A100 training time per job: 45/2.2 = ~20 min - Total time per job: setup + training
COST TABLE:
| GPU | Hourly (spot)| Hourly (OD) | Setup (min) | Train (min) | Total/job (min) | Cost/job (spot) | Total/pass 34 jobs (spot) | |------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|-----------------|---------------------------| | L4 | $0.22 | $0.74 | 10 | 45 | 55 | $0.20 | $6.85 | | A100-40GB | $1.10 | $3.67 | 10 | 20 | 30 | $0.55 | $18.70 | | A100-80GB | $1.38 | $4.61 | 10 | 20 | 30 | $0.69 | $23.46 | | H100 SXM | $3.40 | $9.80 | 10 | 9 | 19 | $1.08 | $36.59 |
COST DELTA: A100-40GB costs $11.85/pass more than L4 spot. But the WAITING COST is $3.22/day (controller VM) + researcher time. After 3.7 days of waiting, the accumulated controller cost alone exceeds the A100 premium for one pass.
RECOMMENDATION: A100-40GB is the right choice. A100-80GB adds $4.76/pass for VRAM that is unnecessary (largest model SAM3 Hybrid uses ~7.5 GB). H100 adds $17.89/pass for speed that is not needed for debug passes.
MAXIMUM COST GUARD: With A100-40GB at spot $1.10/hr and worst-case 1 hour/job, the absolute maximum per-pass cost is 34 x $1.10 = $37.40. This is within the $50-100/pass academic budget.
expert.recommendation: 1. Add ONLY A100-40GB (not A100-80GB, not H100) to the GCP allowlist 2. Set max_hourly_cost_usd for A100 to $1.50 (authorized, with buffer) 3. Keep L4 as primary — A100 is FALLBACK only (ordered: list priority) 4. Consider adding a per-pass budget ceiling to preflight checks

expert.focus: Test suite updates for new GPU allowlist
expert.assessment:
The current test suite has these A100-blocking tests that MUST be updated:
1. `test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100` in test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py — This test was written specifically to prevent the unauthorized A100 incident. It asserts A100 is NOT in gcp allowed list. Must be REMOVED or INVERTED.
2. `test_factorial_uses_exactly_l4` in test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py — Asserts factorial YAML accelerators == ["L4"]. Since the static YAML still says `accelerators: L4:1` (ordered: is injected at runtime), this test should still pass. BUT we need a NEW test that validates the generated ordered: block includes only L4 and A100 (no H100, no A100-80GB).
3. `test_factorial_accelerators_not_dict` — Ensures accelerators is string, not dict. Still valid since the static YAML remains `L4:1`. Pass.
4. `test_factorial_accelerators_match_contract` — Reads factorial.allowed_accelerators from contract. Must be updated in the CONTRACT (not the test) to include A100.
5. Cross-file consistency tests (`test_gcp_skypilot_yamls_match_gcp_spot_config`) — Reads from gcp_spot.yaml. Must update gcp_spot.yaml accelerator list too.
NEW TESTS NEEDED: a. Test that region configs with A100 entries produce valid ordered: blocks b. Test that the generated YAML from region_injection includes only contract-allowed GPUs c. Test that A100 entries appear AFTER all L4 entries in the ordered: list (priority) d. Test preflight cost estimate handles A100 hourly rate correctly
expert.recommendation: 1. Update test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100 to VERIFY A100 IS in gcp list 2. Add test_a100_is_fallback_not_primary — verify L4 entries precede A100 in region configs 3. Add test_region_injection_respects_contract — generated YAML only uses allowed GPUs 4. Add test_preflight_cost_estimate_a100 — cost guardrail works with A100 rates 5. Keep test_factorial_uses_exactly_l4 — the STATIC yaml still says L4:1

expert.focus: YAML contract updates and preflight checks
expert.assessment:
The YAML contract system exists because of the A100 incident. This plan is the FIRST authorized modification to the contract since its creation. The change must be surgically precise and maintain all 5 defense layers:
L1 (Pre-commit): scripts/validate_yaml_contract.py - Reads allowed_accelerators from contract → A100 added to contract → passes - The factorial strict check reads factorial.allowed_accelerators → must update - No code change needed in the validator itself, only in the contract YAML
L2 (Test suite): tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py - test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100 → must be updated (see Test Engineer) - test_factorial_accelerators_match_contract → reads from contract → auto-passes
L3 (Preflight): scripts/preflight_gcp.py - check_yaml_contract() checks accelerators against factorial allowlist - check_gcp_gpu_quota() only checks NVIDIA_L4_GPUS → must add NVIDIA_A100_GPUS - check_cost_estimate() uses max_hourly_cost_usd → A100 already listed (as "NOT AUTHORIZED") → update comment to "AUTHORIZED as fallback" - check_yaml_contract() has a check: "Accelerators is NOT a dict (priority list = non-determinism)" → This check fires when accelerators is a dict. The static YAML keeps accelerators as string "L4:1", and ordered: is injected separately. This check should still pass.
L4 (Experiment harness): Phase 2 VALIDATE gate reads contract → auto-passes
L5 (CLAUDE.md Rule 31): This plan has explicit user authorization. Rule 31 is satisfied.
QUOTA CHECK: GCP GPU quotas are per-type. We need to check NVIDIA_A100_GPUS quota in addition to NVIDIA_L4_GPUS. If A100 quota is 0, the fallback will fail silently (SkyPilot will try A100, get rejected, and exhaust all options). Adding an A100 quota check to preflight prevents this.
expert.recommendation: 1. Update yaml_contract.yaml: add A100 to allowed_accelerators.gcp AND factorial.allowed_accelerators 2. Update max_hourly_cost_usd: change A100 comment from "NOT AUTHORIZED" to authorized 3. Add NVIDIA_A100_GPUS quota check to preflight_gcp.py 4. Keep all 5 defense layers intact — this is a CONTRACT UPDATE, not a bypass 5. Add cost ceiling guard: if estimated total > $50, warn (not block)

expert.assessment:
From a researcher's perspective, this change should be INVISIBLE. The researcher runs the same command:
```bash ./scripts/run_factorial.sh configs/factorial/debug.yaml ```
SkyPilot handles the L4-to-A100 fallback automatically. The researcher sees "Provisioning A100" in the SkyPilot output instead of "PENDING" for 11 hours. This is exactly the DevEx principle: "everything automatic by default, everything tweakable by choice."
The region_config field in debug.yaml controls which regions and GPUs are tried. Changing from `europe_strict` (L4 only, europe-west4 only) to a new config that includes A100 fallback requires updating the infrastructure.region_config field.
CONFIGURATION CLARITY: - `europe_strict.yaml` → L4 only, europe-west4 only (current, gets stuck) - `europe.yaml` → L4 across 3 EU regions (broader, but still L4 only) - NEW: `europe_a100_fallback.yaml` → L4 first across EU, then A100 in europe-west4
The researcher picks their availability/cost tradeoff by setting region_config in their factorial YAML. This is the Hydra config philosophy: choice via config, not code changes.
IMPORTANT: The debug.yaml currently sets `region_config: europe_strict`. To activate the A100 fallback, the user must change this to the new region config. This is a DELIBERATE friction — the researcher explicitly opts into the higher cost tier. We should NOT silently change the default.
expert.recommendation: 1. Create `europe_a100_fallback.yaml` as a NEW region config (do not modify existing) 2. Do NOT change debug.yaml default — let the user choose when to use the fallback 3. Document the cost difference clearly in the region config comments 4. Add a `--gpu-fallback` flag to run_factorial.sh that overrides region_config (future)

council-review.council-synthesis:
All 5 experts agree on the core approach: 1. Add A100-40GB (NOT A100-80GB, NOT H100) to the YAML contract 2. Create a new region config with A100 fallback entries 3. Update tests to reflect the authorized change 4. Add A100 quota check to preflight 5. Keep the static train_factorial.yaml unchanged (ordered: injected at runtime) 6. Let the user explicitly opt in via region_config (not a silent default change)
Key disagreement resolved: Cost Engineer recommended A100-40GB only. Platform Engineer noted A100 (without -40GB/-80GB suffix) is how SkyPilot references it on GCP. In GCP, "A100" means the 40GB variant by default, and "A100-80GB" is the explicit 80GB variant. We use "A100" in configs (maps to 40GB on GCP).

before.scenario: $3.22/day controller burn + $0/pass (no jobs run) — infinite wait

after.scenario: Falls through to next region in ordered: list, then PENDING

cost-impact.breakeven: After 3.7 days of L4 waiting ($3.22/day), the A100 premium is recovered
cost-impact.worst-case: 34 jobs x A100 on-demand x 1 hour = $124.78 — still within $50-100 budget ceiling with spot

risk.description: A100 spot also exhausted in europe-west4
risk.mitigation: Region config includes europe-west1 as secondary region for A100

risk.description: A100 quota is 0 (never requested for this GCP project)
risk.mitigation: Preflight check for NVIDIA_A100_GPUS quota added (Task 4). User must request quota via GCP console if 0.

risk.description: A100 training produces different results than L4 (numerical precision)
risk.mitigation: Both A100 (Ampere) and L4 (Ada Lovelace) support BF16. Results should be equivalent within floating point tolerance. This is a debug pass — exact reproducibility is not the goal.

risk.description: Accidental A100 on-demand (spot unavailable, SkyPilot falls back to on-demand)
risk.mitigation: train_factorial.yaml has `use_spot: true`. SkyPilot will NOT fall back to on-demand unless `--no-use-spot` is passed (only for SAM3 models). Cost guard: max_hourly_cost_usd in contract.

risk.description: GAR Docker image pull from A100 VM in different zone takes longer
risk.mitigation: A100 in europe-west4 = same region as GAR. Cross-region pull (europe-west1) adds ~5-10 min but is acceptable vs 11+ hr PENDING.

rollback-plan.description: If A100 causes unexpected issues (cost overrun, training failures, quota problems), revert to L4-only by:

steps.step: 2. Revert yaml_contract.yaml: remove A100 from allowed_accelerators.gcp and factorial.allowed_accelerators
3. Revert gcp_spot.yaml: remove A100 from accelerators list
6. Run `make test-staging` to verify all tests pass with reverted config

rollback-plan.time-to-revert: 5 minutes — all changes are config/test only, no code changes

task.name: Update YAML Contract
task.description: Add A100 (40GB) to the golden contract as an authorized GCP fallback GPU. Update factorial section to allow both L4 and A100. Update cost guardrails to reflect authorized status.

change.description: Add A100 to GCP allowed accelerators
change.after: allowed_accelerators: gcp: - L4 - A100 # Fallback for L4 capacity exhaustion — authorized 2026-03-28

change.description: Add A100 to factorial allowed accelerators
change.after: factorial: allowed_accelerators: - L4 - A100 # Ordered fallback — L4 primary, A100 when L4 exhausted

change.before: A100: 1.50 # NOT AUTHORIZED — listed only for detection A100-80GB: 1.80 # NOT AUTHORIZED H100: 3.00 # NOT AUTHORIZED
change.after: A100: 1.50 # AUTHORIZED as fallback — GCP spot ~$1.10/hr (2026-03-28) A100-80GB: 1.80 # NOT AUTHORIZED — 40GB sufficient for all models H100: 3.00 # NOT AUTHORIZED — overpowered for debug passes

verification-criteria.criterion: yaml_contract.yaml has A100 in allowed_accelerators.gcp
yaml_contract.yaml has A100 in factorial.allowed_accelerators
A100 max_hourly_cost_usd comment says AUTHORIZED
A100-80GB and H100 remain NOT AUTHORIZED
validate_yaml_contract.py passes on all SkyPilot YAMLs

task.name: Create A100 Fallback Region Config
task.description: Create a new region config file that includes both L4 and A100 entries, with L4 entries first (higher priority) and A100 entries second (fallback). The region_injection.py module already handles multi-GPU region configs with zero code changes needed.

change.content:
# EU regions with A100 fallback — use when L4 spot is capacity-exhausted. # # Priority order (enforced by region_injection.py iteration order): # 1. L4 in europe-west4 — cheapest ($0.22/hr spot), co-located with GAR/GCS # 2. L4 in europe-west1 — second cheapest, low cross-region egress # 3. L4 in europe-west3 — third EU region # 4. A100 in europe-west4 — fallback ($1.10/hr spot), same region as infra # 5. A100 in europe-west1 — secondary A100 region # # User authorization: 2026-03-28 (gpu-instances-finops-report.md Recommendation 1) # Cost impact: A100 debug pass = ~$18.70 vs L4 at ~$6.85 (+$11.85) # Breakeven: 3.7 days of L4 waiting ($3.22/day controller) > A100 premium # # See: docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/gpu-instances-finops-report.md # See: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-24-unauthorized-a100-in-skypilot-yaml.md
description: "EU regions with A100 fallback — L4 primary, A100 when L4 exhausted"
regions: gcp: L4: - region: europe-west4 # Netherlands — co-located with GAR/GCS zones: 3 - region: europe-west1 # Belgium — low cross-region egress zones: 2 - region: europe-west3 # Frankfurt — third EU region zones: 2 A100: - region: europe-west4 # Netherlands — same region as infra zones: 3 - region: europe-west1 # Belgium — secondary A100 region zones: 2

command.description: Verify region injection produces valid output
Verify L4 entries precede A100 entries in generated YAML

test-commands.command: uv run python -c " import yaml from pathlib import Path generated = yaml.safe_load(Path('/tmp/test_region_injection/generated_train_factorial.yaml').read_text()) ordered = generated['resources']['ordered'] gpu_order = [entry['accelerators'].split(':')[0] for entry in ordered] l4_indices = [i for i, g in enumerate(gpu_order) if g == 'L4'] a100_indices = [i for i, g in enumerate(gpu_order) if g == 'A100'] assert max(l4_indices) < min(a100_indices), f'L4 must precede A100: {gpu_order}' print(f'GPU ordering correct: {gpu_order}') "

verification-criteria.criterion: europe_a100_fallback.yaml exists and parses without errors
region_injection produces ordered: block with L4 entries before A100 entries
Generated YAML is valid SkyPilot syntax
Only L4 and A100 appear in the generated ordered: block (no H100, no A100-80GB)

task.name: Update Cloud Config
task.description: Add A100 to the gcp_spot.yaml accelerator list. This file is read by cross-file consistency tests to verify SkyPilot YAMLs only use cloud-config-approved GPUs.

change.before: accelerators: - L4:1 # A100 REMOVED — was never authorized by user (5.5x cost). # Add back ONLY with explicit user authorization.
change.after: accelerators: - L4:1 - A100:1 # Fallback for L4 capacity exhaustion — authorized 2026-03-28 # A100-80GB NOT AUTHORIZED — 40GB sufficient for all models # H100 NOT AUTHORIZED — overpowered for debug passes

verification-criteria.criterion: gcp_spot.yaml accelerators list contains both L4:1 and A100:1
Cross-file consistency tests pass (SkyPilot YAML GPUs subset of cloud config GPUs)

task.name: Update Preflight Checks
task.description: Add A100 GPU quota check to preflight_gcp.py. Update the YAML contract validator check that currently rejects dict-format accelerators (the static YAML is unchanged, but verify the preflight handles ordered: blocks from generated YAMLs correctly). Update cost estimate to handle A100 rates.

change.detail: Add a new preflight check function `check_gcp_a100_gpu_quota()` that queries NVIDIA_A100_GPUS quota in europe-west4 and europe-west1. If quota is 0, WARN (not block) — the user may need to request quota. Structure mirrors existing `check_gcp_gpu_quota()` function.

change.description: Update cost estimate to show A100 fallback cost
change.detail: Update `check_cost_estimate()` to: 1. Read the region_config from the factorial config 2. If region config includes A100, show BOTH cost estimates: - Best case (L4): $6.85/pass - Fallback (A100): $18.70/pass 3. Show cost ceiling based on max_hourly_cost_usd

change.description: Register A100 quota check in main()
change.detail: Add `("GCP A100 GPU quota", check_gcp_a100_gpu_quota)` to the checks list in main(). This runs alongside the existing L4 quota check.

command.description: Verify preflight script runs without error (will skip gcloud checks locally)

verification-criteria.criterion: preflight_gcp.py has check_gcp_a100_gpu_quota function
check_gcp_a100_gpu_quota queries NVIDIA_A100_GPUS metric
cost estimate shows A100 fallback cost when region config includes A100
A100 quota check registered in main() checks list

task.name: Update Tests
task.description: Update the YAML contract enforcement test suite to reflect the authorized A100 addition. Remove the test that blocks A100 in GCP. Add new tests for A100 fallback ordering and region config validation.

change.detail: REPLACE this test with `test_contract_gcp_includes_authorized_a100`: - Assert A100 IS in gcp allowed list (it is now authorized) - Assert A100-80GB is NOT in gcp allowed list (not authorized) - Assert H100 is NOT in gcp allowed list (not authorized) - Add provenance comment: "A100 authorized 2026-03-28, FinOps report Recommendation 1"

This test asserts `unique_gpus == ["L4"]` for the STATIC factorial YAML. Since the static YAML still says `accelerators: L4:1`, this test should still pass WITHOUT changes. Verify this is the case.
However, update the factorial.allowed_accelerators in the contract (T1.2) means the contract-based test `test_factorial_accelerators_match_contract` now allows both L4 and A100. This is CORRECT — the static YAML uses L4, which is a subset of {L4, A100}. No test change needed.

New test class `TestA100FallbackOrdering`:
a. `test_a100_fallback_region_config_exists` — verify europe_a100_fallback.yaml exists b. `test_l4_entries_precede_a100_entries` — in every region config that includes A100, ALL L4 entries must appear before ANY A100 entry (priority ordering) c. `test_region_config_gpus_match_contract` — every GPU type in region configs must be in the YAML contract allowed_accelerators for that cloud d. `test_generated_yaml_respects_gpu_ordering` — run region_injection and verify the generated ordered: block has L4 before A100

Parametrized test over all region config files in configs/cloud/regions/: - For each region config, extract all GPU types - Verify each GPU type is in the YAML contract allowed_accelerators for the cloud - Verify no banned GPU types appear This catches future unauthorized GPU additions to region configs.

command.description: RED phase: run existing tests to see which fail after contract change
GREEN phase: run all contract tests after test updates

verification-criteria.criterion: test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100 replaced with test that VERIFIES A100 is authorized
test_factorial_uses_exactly_l4 still passes (static YAML unchanged)
New test_a100_fallback_ordering_in_region_configs passes
New test_no_unauthorized_gpus_in_region_configs passes
All existing YAML contract tests pass (no regressions)
make test-staging passes with 0 failures, 0 unexpected skips

task.name: Update Preflight YAML Contract Validator
task.description:
The preflight check_yaml_contract() function in scripts/preflight_gcp.py has a check that rejects dict-format accelerators as "non-deterministic." This was added after the A100 incident. Since the static train_factorial.yaml still uses string format `L4:1` (ordered: is injected at runtime), this check should still pass. Verify and document.
Also update the validate_yaml_contract.py pre-commit hook to handle ordered: blocks that contain A100 (currently passes because ordered: blocks are validated against the contract allowlist, and A100 is now in the contract).

change.description: Verify validate_yaml_contract.py handles A100 in ordered: blocks
change.detail: The validator already checks ordered: blocks in the validate_yaml() function (lines 135-156). It validates each option's accelerators against the allowed_accelerators for that cloud. Since A100 is now in the contract, this will pass. No code change needed — just VERIFY with a test.

Update the comment in check_yaml_contract() that says: "Accelerators is NOT a dict (priority list = non-determinism)" to clarify this only applies to the STATIC YAML, not to generated ordered: blocks. The static YAML remains `accelerators: L4:1`.

command.description: Verify pre-commit validator passes on all SkyPilot YAMLs
Verify pre-commit validator accepts generated YAML with A100 in ordered

test-commands.command:
uv run python -c " from pathlib import Path from scripts.validate_yaml_contract import load_contract, validate_yaml import yaml, tempfile
# Generate a YAML that looks like what region_injection produces generated = { 'name': 'test', 'resources': { 'image_id': 'docker:test', 'cloud': 'gcp', 'use_spot': True, 'disk_size': 100, 'disk_tier': 'best', 'ordered': [ {'accelerators': 'L4:1', 'region': 'europe-west4'}, {'accelerators': 'A100:1', 'region': 'europe-west4'}, ], }, 'envs': {}, 'setup': 'echo test', 'run': 'echo test', } with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.yaml', mode='w', delete=False) as f: yaml.dump(generated, f) tmp_path = Path(f.name)
contract = load_contract() violations = validate_yaml(tmp_path, contract) tmp_path.unlink() assert not violations, f'Violations: {violations}' print('Generated YAML with A100 in ordered: block passes validation') "

verification-criteria.criterion: validate_yaml_contract.py passes on all existing SkyPilot YAMLs
validate_yaml_contract.py accepts generated YAML with A100 in ordered: blocks
preflight check_yaml_contract() still passes on static train_factorial.yaml

task.name: Update Knowledge Graph and Documentation
task.description: Update the cloud domain KG to reflect the A100 fallback decision. Update CLAUDE.md if needed. The KG already has `prod` environment listing "GCP L4/A100 spot" — verify this is accurate.

change.description: Add A100 fallback decision to cloud.yaml
change.detail:
Add a new decision entry under `decisions:`:
```yaml a100_capacity_fallback: status: resolved winner: ordered_l4_a100 rationale: > L4 capacity exhaustion in europe-west4 (11+ hours PENDING, 2026-03-28). A100-40GB added as ordered fallback via region config. L4 remains primary. Cost: $18.70/pass vs $6.85 for L4 (+$11.85). Breakeven vs waiting: 3.7 days. User authorized 2026-03-28 after reviewing FinOps report. implementation: - configs/cloud/yaml_contract.yaml - configs/cloud/regions/europe_a100_fallback.yaml - configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml planning_doc: docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/gpu-instances-finops-report.md metalearning: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-24-unauthorized-a100-in-skypilot-yaml.md ```

change.description: Verify cloud.yaml environments section is accurate
change.detail: The `staging` environment already says `compute: "GCP L4 spot"`. The `prod` environment already says `compute: "GCP L4/A100 spot"`. Update `staging` to also include A100 as fallback: `compute: "GCP L4 spot (A100 fallback via ordered: regions)"`
CLAUDE.md already says "GCP L4/A100 spot" in the environment table for staging/prod. Verify this is still accurate after the changes. No change needed if already present.

verification-criteria.criterion: cloud.yaml has a100_capacity_fallback decision with resolved status
cloud.yaml environments section reflects A100 fallback for staging
KG integrity tests pass

phase.name: Contract and Config Changes
phase.description: Update the golden contract, create the region config, and update the cloud config. These are the config-only changes that enable A100 fallback.

tdd-sequence.step: RED: Run existing test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100 — it will PASS (A100 not yet in contract)
RED: Write new test_contract_gcp_includes_authorized_a100 — it will FAIL (A100 not yet in contract)
RED: Write new test_a100_fallback_region_config_exists — it will FAIL (file not yet created)
GREEN: Apply T1 changes (yaml_contract.yaml)
GREEN: Apply T2 changes (create europe_a100_fallback.yaml)
GREEN: Apply T3 changes (gcp_spot.yaml)
VERIFY: Run all YAML contract tests — should pass

phase.name: Test Suite Updates
phase.description: Update the test suite to reflect the authorized A100 addition. Replace blocking tests with verification tests.

tdd-sequence.step: RED: Run test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100 — it will FAIL (A100 now in contract)
GREEN: Replace with test_contract_gcp_includes_authorized_a100
GREEN: Add test_a100_fallback_ordering_in_region_configs
GREEN: Add test_no_unauthorized_gpus_in_region_configs
VERIFY: make test-staging — 0 failures, 0 unexpected skips

phase.name: Preflight and Validator Updates
phase.description: Update preflight checks and the pre-commit validator to handle A100. Add A100 quota check.

tdd-sequence.step: RED: Write test for check_gcp_a100_gpu_quota function — it will FAIL (function not yet written)
GREEN: Implement check_gcp_a100_gpu_quota in preflight_gcp.py
GREEN: Update check_cost_estimate to show A100 fallback cost
VERIFY: Run preflight script — should show A100 quota check in output
VERIFY: Run validate_yaml_contract.py — should accept A100 in ordered: blocks

phase.name: Knowledge Graph Update
phase.description: Update cloud.yaml KG domain with the A100 fallback decision. Documentation-level change, lowest risk.

tdd-sequence.step: GREEN: Add a100_capacity_fallback decision to cloud.yaml
GREEN: Update environment descriptions
VERIFY: KG integrity tests pass

phase.name: Final Verification
phase.description: Full test suite run to confirm zero regressions.

tdd-sequence.step: VERIFY: make test-staging — 0 failures, 0 unexpected skips
VERIFY: uv run python scripts/validate_yaml_contract.py --all — passes
VERIFY: uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py — shows A100 quota check
VERIFY: Dry-run factorial with new region config: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh --dry-run configs/factorial/debug.yaml (after temporarily setting region_config: europe_a100_fallback in debug.yaml)

table.name: Monthly Scenario Analysis (4 debug passes + 1 production)

out-of-scope.item: Add Vast.ai as a cloud provider (Phase 2 of FinOps report — separate plan)
Add Nebius as a cloud provider (Phase 3 of FinOps report — separate plan)
Add H100 to the GPU allowlist (not needed for debug passes)
Add A100-80GB to the GPU allowlist (40GB sufficient for all models)
Change the default region_config in debug.yaml (user opts in explicitly)
Modify train_factorial.yaml static YAML (ordered: injected at runtime)
Modify run_factorial.sh (already handles ordered: via region injection)
Modify region_injection.py (already handles multi-GPU region configs)
Add --gpu-fallback flag to run_factorial.sh (future DevEx improvement)
